title: Why are we getting frustrated? | HG Amitasana Prabhu
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: Our desire to be the Lord, to be the enjoyer, to achieve permanence will always be frustrated
because Krishna declares this material world is a prison house, is dukha alayam ashashvatam.
Just like Himalaya, Himalaya means, Him means barf, snow, a place which is full of snow.
So, granth alaya, a place which is full of granth.
Anath alaya, a place where orphans are there.
Dukha alaya, a place which is full of dukha.
Now somebody says, no, no, you are saying dukha, you know, He is happy.
Yes, between some dukha, some sukhah is also packed.
But that sukhah is ashashvatam.
It's temporary.
So in that sense, it is like a prison house.
Our desire is to live a life of permanence, to live a life which is full of bliss, enjoyment, full of knowledge.
And that's why Krishna says,
Our desire for that life is getting frustrated at every step.
And we are getting frustrated after many, many births.
A person in knowledge, a person who is little bit wise, he will say, why am I suffering?
Why this happiness which I want to achieve is elusive?
Why there is so much of struggle in this world?
Actually, animals cannot figure out, neither they can ask these questions.
Somebody, when we ask this question, why am I suffering?
He is a pessimist.
He is not strong.
He is scared of life.
He is a pessimist.
Actually, it is not.
Real knowledge means to understand, why am I suffering?
Who am I?
When a person starts,
Bahu naam, Janma naam ante,
Gyan van, a person who is in knowledge, who is sufficiently experienced,
Maam prapadyaante.
Krishna says, will come to me, will surrender to me.
Now, this is not a binary logic.
Zero or one.
Surrender to means, we suddenly become a pure devotee.
No.
We pass through a stage where we become little awakened.
We realize that yes, the Lord is there.
Lord has to be glorified.
Lord has to be served.
I am not the Lord.
the lord i am the servant of the lord we realize that but we are filled with material desires
